data.title: Add runtime budget guidance to red-test-writer and red-test-evaluator agents
data.description:
## Context
Developers using DSO's TDD workflow rely on the red-test-writer agent to produce fast-feedback RED tests. Currently, the agent has no runtime guidance, so it can produce unit tests that run excessively long — slowing the edit-test cycle and potentially hitting the ~73s Claude Code tool timeout ceiling. The red-test-evaluator, which triages writer rejections, also lacks any runtime check, leaving no safety net for slow tests.

## Success Criteria
1. The red-test-writer agent estimates both RED-phase and GREEN-phase runtime before writing a unit test, expressed as a positive integer number of seconds, and includes both estimates in its output via ESTIMATED_RUNTIME_RED and ESTIMATED_RUNTIME_GREEN fields
2. The agent's estimated runtime fields for unit tests must not exceed 10 seconds; when estimates would exceed 10 seconds the agent must restructure or reject rather than emit the test. Integration and E2E tests are exempt. A test is treated as a unit test if it has no network calls, no subprocess spawning, and no real filesystem I/O beyond temporary directories
3. When a unit test's estimated runtime would exceed 10 seconds, the agent documents at least one restructuring approach it considered (mocking expensive operations, timing functions, or reducing dependencies) and applies it if feasible to bring the estimate within budget
4. When restructuring cannot bring a unit test under 10 seconds, the agent rejects with a clear justification explaining why the test must exceed the limit, using the existing TEST_RESULT:rejected format
5. The red-test-evaluator issues a REVISE verdict when a unit test's estimated runtime exceeds 10 seconds and includes a runtime-specific note in its rationale. When ESTIMATED_RUNTIME fields are absent (pre-epic outputs), the evaluator treats runtime as unknown and does not block the verdict
6. At least one eval test case presents the red-test-writer with a scenario containing a known-slow operation and confirms the agent either restructures it or rejects it; a corresponding eval for the red-test-evaluator presents a test with obvious slow operation and asserts REVISE rather than CONFIRM

## Dependencies
None — red-test-writer and red-test-evaluator agent specs are stable and unmodified by open tickets. The REVISE/REJECT/CONFIRM verdict schema requires no structural changes, only additive runtime-flag logic.

## Approach
Add a new "Section 8: Runtime Budget" to red-test-writer.md with estimation protocol, restructuring strategies (mock expensive ops, timing functions, reduce dependencies), and fallback-to-rejection flow. Extend output contract with ESTIMATED_RUNTIME_RED and ESTIMATED_RUNTIME_GREEN fields (positive integer seconds). Add runtime awareness section to red-test-evaluator.md with backward-compatible handling of missing fields.

## Scenario Analysis
- LLM runtime estimation consistently underestimates I/O-bound test durations (high, runtime): The agent's estimation relies on judgment with no execution feedback loop. Tests involving disk I/O or subprocess spawning may be systematically underestimated.
- Timing-function mock causes GREEN-phase test to never terminate (high, runtime): Patching time.sleep to no-op while code uses busy-wait on wall time can cause infinite loops in GREEN phase.
- Existing red-test-writer outputs without ESTIMATED_RUNTIME fields break red-test-evaluator (high, deployment): In-flight workflows may pass old-format outputs to the updated evaluator. Addressed by SC5 backward-compatibility clause.
- ESTIMATED_RUNTIME fields accept free-form text (high, configuration): Without strict numeric format, evaluator parsing fails. Addressed by SC1 — positive integer seconds.
- Restructuring may mock dependencies RED tests rely on (high, configuration): Mocking for runtime may accidentally make RED tests pass for wrong reasons, violating TDD invariant.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 5
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
